question_title: Will Meta publicly announce or release a model explicitly named “Llama 5” before April 30, 2026?
summary: Meta's next-generation model (codenamed "Avocado") has been delayed from Q1 2026 to at least May 2026, pushing it past the April 30 deadline. Furthermore, reports indicate Avocado may abandon the Llama branding entirely in favor of a closed-source approach. No separate "Llama 5" project exists. Manifold gives only 8% for Llama 5 before April 2026. With Meta in organizational turmoil (massive layoffs, leadership changes), a surprise "Llama 5" announcement before April 30 is very unlikely.

factors.description: Avocado (Meta's next model) delayed from Q1 2026 to at least May 2026 - past the April 30 deadline. Confirmed by NYT, Reuters, Bloomberg as of March 13, 2026.

Avocado may not be branded 'Llama 5'. Reports describe it as a successor that may abandon Llama branding and go closed-source. CNBC headline: 'From Llamas to Avocados'. Question requires explicit 'Llama 5' branding.
Manifold market gives only 14% for Llama 5 before May 2026 and 8% before April 2026. These use looser resolution criteria than this question.
Meta in organizational turmoil: up to 20% layoffs planned, Llama 4 Behemoth shelved, Yann LeCun departed, new AI leadership still settling in. Not conducive to accelerated release.
No separate 'Llama 5' project reported - Avocado is the only next-gen text model. Annual cadence from Llama 4 (April 2025) would suggest April 2026 at earliest, but the delay pushes beyond that.
Small residual possibility: Meta could surprise-announce a 'Llama 5' branded model or rebrand a variant. But no evidence supports this scenario.

reasoning:
**Final forecast: 4% probability (logit: -3.2)**

The evidence strongly points to NO. Meta's successor model "Avocado" has been delayed to at least May 2026 (past the April 30 deadline), and it may not even carry the "Llama 5" branding — reports suggest Meta is moving away from the Llama brand toward closed-source development. With no other "Llama 5" project reported and Meta in organizational upheaval, a surprise announcement within 36 days is very unlikely.

resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if Meta publicly announces or releases a model explicitly branded “Llama 5” before April 30, 2026 on the Llama page at https://ai.meta.com/llama or the Meta AI blog at https://ai.meta.com/blog/. It resolves to no otherwise.
fine_print: The branding must explicitly include “Llama 5”. References to Llama 4 variants, rumors, code names, or third-party reports do not qualify.
